hypothesis: If pre-trained single-gas neural networks are used as frozen feature extractors and their outputs concatenated with gas fractions to form node attributes for a GraphSAGE-based two-node graph, then this architecture will yield more accurate and better-generalizing predictions of radial etch-rate profiles for Ar/O2 mixtures—including extrapolation to withheld high power/pressure and low-Ar-fraction regimes—than a conventional FNN baseline and alternative GNN approaches, because the frozen single-gas features embed gas-specific physics that improve mixture representation.
